name: Jagadguru Ramabhadracharya
dates_notes: Living jagadguru; born 14 January 1950 in Shandikhurd, Jaunpur district, Uttar Pradesh; blind from age two months and never used Braille (composes by dictation); appointed Jagadguru Rāmānandācārya 1988, ritually anointed 1 August 1995; head of Tulsī Pīṭha, Citrakūṭa. dates_high field set to 2026 by current-year convention; thinker is living.

dates_evidence.description: Government of India Padma awards records: Padma Vibhūṣaṇa (2015), Padma Bhūṣaṇa (earlier).
Tulsī Pīṭha publication colophons; release ceremony of Śrīrāghavakṛpā-Bhāṣyam by Prime Minister A. B. Vajpayee, 10 April 1998.
Rāmānanda Sampradāya jagadguru-anointment records, Tulsī Pīṭha Sevā Nyāsa, 1 August 1995.

core_thesis: Rāmabhadrācārya, jagadguru of the Rāmānanda Sampradāya, gives Viśiṣṭādvaita a Rāma-centric formulation. The *parabrahman* of the Upaniṣads is *Sītā-Rāma* (*Śrīrāghavakṛpā-Bhāṣyam* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.1, *athāto brahma-jijñāsā*; with prefatory *maṅgala*-verses); the *śarīra-śarīri-bhāva* of Rāmānuja is preserved with Rāma as *śarīrin* and *cit* and *acit* as his real body (*ŚRKB* on BS 2.1.9, *vaiṣamya-nairghṛṇye na sāpekṣatvāt*; on BS 1.4.27, *atrāntare brahma-yogyatvāt*). His *Śrīrāghavakṛpā-Bhāṣyam* (released 1998) is the first Sanskrit commentary on the entire *prasthāna-trayī* in roughly five centuries and the second in the Rāmānanda Sampradāya after Rāmānanda's own *Ānanda-Bhāṣya* (*ŚRKB* general Introduction; Tulsī Pīṭha 1998 release ceremony documentation). Methodologically he insists that Rāma is the supreme reality (*pratipādya*) of every Upaniṣad and every *sūtra* of Bādarāyaṇa (*ŚRKB* on BS 1.1.4, *tat tu samanvayāt*; *ŚRKB* on BS 4.4.22, *anāvṛttiḥ śabdāt*), reading the *mahā-vākyas* through the body-soul template Rāmānuja established (*ŚRKB on Chāndogyopaniṣad* 6.8.7 *tat tvam asi*; *ŚRKB on Bṛhadāraṇyaka* 1.4.10 *aham brahmāsmi*). His engagement with the school's formal architecture is therefore continuous with Vaḍakalai Viśiṣṭādvaita on the metaphysics of *apṛthak-siddhi*; the distinctive move is the Rāma-iṣṭadevatā exegesis. Blind from infancy and never having used Braille, he composes by dictation. His oeuvre — 240+ books in Sanskrit, Hindi, and other languages — includes a verse-commentary on Pāṇini's *Aṣṭādhyāyī* (*Aṣṭādhyāyī-Pradīpa*), four Sanskrit *mahā-kāvyas* (*Śrīsītārāmasuprabhātam*; *Ārṣatrayam*; *Bhṛṅgadūtam*; *Kākā-Vidura*), and Hindi commentaries on Tulsīdās.

engaged_works.title: Shri-Raghava-Kripa-Bhashyam on Brahma-Sutra
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across the 1990s; released as part of the prasthāna-trayī set on 10 April 1998 by Prime Minister A. B. Vajpayee.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Sanskrit commentary; Rāmānanda Sampradāya school commentary on the Brahma-Sūtra.

ascription_evidence.description: Tulsī Pīṭha publication colophon, 1998 first edition.

engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit *bhāṣya* on Bādarāyaṇa's *Brahma-Sūtra*, organised by the four *adhyāyas* and their *pādas* in the standard manner. The interpretive principle is fixed at the outset (*ŚRKB on Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.1, *athāto brahma-jijñāsā*, with the prefatory *maṅgala*-verses on Sītā-Rāma): every *sūtra* whose *pratipādya* is the supreme Brahman is read as referring to Sītā-Rāma, the *parabrahman* of the Rāmānanda Sampradāya. The body-soul (*śarīra-śarīrin*) template of Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* is preserved without revision; Rāma is the *śarīrin*, *cit* and *acit* are his real *śarīra* in *apṛthak-siddha* relation (*ŚRKB on BS* 1.4.23, *prakṛtiś ca pratijñā-dṛṣṭāntānuparodhāt*; 2.1.9, on the *vaiṣamya-nairghṛṇye* objection). The *samanvaya-adhyāya* (BS 1.1–1.4) is read as fixing this Rāma-centric *tātparya* across Upaniṣadic loci (notably *ŚRKB* on BS 1.1.4 *tat tu samanvayāt*; 1.2.1–8 *sarvatra-prasiddhi-adhikaraṇa*); the *avirodha-adhyāya* (BS 2.1–2.4) defends the position against rival *darśana*-s (*ŚRKB* on BS 2.2.1ff., on Sāṅkhya, Yoga, Vaiśeṣika, and Buddhist refutations); the *sādhanādhyāya* (BS 3) and *phalādhyāya* (BS 4) treat the path and the goal in the Vaiṣṇava devotional register (*ŚRKB* on BS 3.4.26 *sarvāpekṣā ca yajñādi-śruteḥ*; 4.4.22 *anāvṛttiḥ śabdāt*). The work is described in the school as the second Sanskrit *bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtra* in the Rāmānanda lineage after Rāmānanda's own *Ānanda-Bhāṣya* (*ŚRKB* general Introduction, on the lineage history), and the first comprehensive *prasthāna-trayī* commentary in Sanskrit in roughly five centuries.

engaged_works.title: Shri-Raghava-Kripa-Bhashyam on Bhagavad-Gita
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across the 1990s; released as part of the prasthāna-trayī set on 10 April 1998.

ascription_evidence.description: Tulsī Pīṭha publication colophon, 1998 first edition.

engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on the *Bhagavad-Gītā* in the same exegetical mode as the *Brahma-Sūtra* commentary. The supreme person of chapters 10, 11, and 15 — *puruṣottama* — is identified with Rāma rather than with Kṛṣṇa-as-fount (*ŚRKB on Gītā* 10.8 *ahaṃ sarvasya prabhavaḥ*; 11.18 *tvam akṣaraṃ paramaṃ veditavyam*; 15.16–18 *dvāv imau puruṣau loke*); the *avatāra*-doctrine of chapter 4 is read as making Rāma and Kṛṣṇa successive descents of one *parabrahman* (*ŚRKB on Gītā* 4.6–8 *yadā yadā hi dharmasya*). On the *karma*–*jñāna*–*bhakti* triad the commentary takes the standard Viśiṣṭādvaita position that *bhakti-yoga* is the integrative path, with *karma* and *jñāna* as its constituent *aṅga*-s rather than rivals (*ŚRKB on Gītā* 3.3 *jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṃ*; 12.1–12 the *bhakti-jñāna* comparison). The chapter-18 surrender-verse (*sarva-dharmān parityajya* — Gītā 18.66) is read as enjoining *prapatti*-grade total surrender to the supreme person (*ŚRKB on Gītā* 18.66, *carama-śloka-vyākhyā*), in continuity with Rāmānuja's *Gadya-traya* (*Śaraṇāgati-Gadya*). The commentary is part of the 1998 *prasthāna-trayī* release and is treated within the Sampradāya as the school's modern Gītā-reference.

engaged_works.title: Shri-Raghava-Kripa-Bhashyam on Eleven Principal Upanishads
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across the 1990s; released as part of the prasthāna-trayī set on 10 April 1998.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Sanskrit commentaries on the eleven principal Upaniṣads.

ascription_evidence.description: Tulsī Pīṭha publication colophon, 1998 first edition.

engaged_works.summary: Sanskrit *bhāṣyas* on the eleven principal Upaniṣads — Īśa, Kena, Kaṭha, Praśna, Muṇḍaka, Māṇḍūkya, Aitareya, Taittirīya, Chāndogya, Bṛhadāraṇyaka, and Śvetāśvatara — bundled here as a single work-entry. The interpretive method is the Rāma-centric Viśiṣṭādvaita exegesis applied uniformly across the corpus. The *mahā-vākyas* (*tat tvam asi*, *aham brahmāsmi*, *prajñānam brahma*) are read through Rāmānuja's *ghaṭaka-śruti*-mediated mode of identity-predication: identity is the identity of the *śarīrin* and the *śarīra*, not flat numerical identity. The Praśna and Muṇḍaka treatments emphasise the *parā* / *aparā vidyā* distinction and the *iṣṭāpūrta*-passage. The Māṇḍūkya is treated, against the Gauḍapāda-Śaṅkara reading, as compatible with the realist Viśiṣṭādvaita ontology. The Bṛhadāraṇyaka commentary, the longest of the set, contains the school's modern handling of the *antaryāmin-brāhmaṇa* — the locus on which the school's *śarīra-śarīrin* doctrine has always rested.

engaged_works.title: Shri-Raghava-Kripa-Bhashyam on Narada-Bhakti-Sutra
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Released as part of the 1998 prasthāna-trayī set, with the Nārada-Bhakti-Sūtra commentary appended.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Sanskrit commentary on Nārada's Bhakti-Sūtra.

ascription_evidence.description: Tulsī Pīṭha publication colophon, 1998 first edition.

engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on the eighty-four *sūtras* of the *Nārada-Bhakti-Sūtra*. The treatise establishes *bhakti* as *parā* — supreme love — and as both the *upāya* and the *upeya* on the school's reading. Rāmabhadrācārya systematises the eleven-fold *sādhana*-classification (*guṇa-māhātmya-āsakti*, *rūpāsakti*, and so on) by mapping each sub-mode onto an exemplary Rāmāyaṇa episode, so that Bharata, Hanumān, Lakṣmaṇa, the *vānara* host, and Sītā together exhibit the full devotional taxonomy. The work integrates the Sampradāya's dual register — Sanskrit Vedāntic exegesis on one side, Rāmāyaṇa-centred *bhakti*-pedagogy on the other — and shows the systematic continuity between Rāmānuja's *Gadya-traya* surrender-doctrine and the Rāmānanda lineage's later devotional practice.

engaged_works.title: Ashtadhyayi-Pradipa
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across the late 20th and early 21st century.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Sanskrit verse-commentary on Pāṇini's Aṣṭādhyāyī.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit verse-commentary on Pāṇini's *Aṣṭādhyāyī*. The work matters here, in a Vedāntic register, because it underwrites the philological discipline on which the *Śrīrāghavakṛpā-Bhāṣyam* exegesis depends. Rāmabhadrācārya's interpretive practice rests on close grammatical analysis of *sūtra*-compounds and Upaniṣadic predication, and the *Aṣṭādhyāyī-Pradīpa* is the workshop in which that grammatical apparatus is laid out. The treatment of *kāraka*-relations, of compound resolution by the appropriate *samāsa*-type, and of the *lakāra*-system is conducted with the explicit aim of supporting Vedāntic exegesis rather than as an independent grammarian's project. Included in this corpus as evidence of his Sanskrit competence rather than as a Vedāntic text proper; treat as a methodological appendix to the *prasthāna-trayī* corpus, on which the school's modern exegetical authority partly rests.
